title: invAIriant Finding
description: A single audit finding. Core rule: no evidence, no finding — a finding with an empty or vague evidence list is invalid. Unsupported claims belong in the report's observations/hypotheses/open-questions sections, not here. S0/S1 findings require high or medium confidence.

id.description: Stable finding id, e.g. INV-001. The prefix is project-configurable (see invairiant.config.schema.json).

severity.description: S0 blocks merge/release/phase transition; S1 must be fixed before the next major step; S2 next work cycle; S3 low; NOTE observation with no mandatory action.

lens.description: Lens id in kebab-case (e.g. 'parnas', 'oracle-boundary') or its display name ('Parnas').

category.description: Optional named finding category from the project's registry, e.g. SINGLE_POINT_OF_FAILURE, SILENT_DEGRADATION. Named categories carry a default severity.

claim.description: One falsifiable sentence stating the defect. Not a feeling, not a question.

evidence.description: Concrete, independently checkable evidence. At least one item is mandatory.

risk.description: What concretely goes wrong if the finding is not addressed, and for whom.

confidence.description: Low-confidence items must normally be recorded as observations, not findings.

status.description: Pipeline status. Lens auditors emit candidates; only the evidence-verification step may set 'verified'; rejected candidates are kept in the report's Unsupported Hypotheses section.

method.description: How the evidence was checked: file read, command re-run, test executed, doc cross-read.

source.description: Who produced the finding. AI-proposed findings must pass evidence verification before counting.

links.description: Related findings, issues, ADRs, or incident reports.

evidence.description: One independently checkable piece of evidence. 'Looks risky', 'probably overcomplicated', and 'AI may have generated this' are not evidence types.

commit.description: Commit hash the file/lines reference was taken at.

doc.description: Doc path (+optional :lines) whose claim the code contradicts.

description.description: What is missing/mismatched and why it matters.

reference.description: CI run URL/id, or incident report id/link.

schema.description: Schema path/ref in a schema/config mismatch.

config.description: Config path/ref in a schema/config mismatch.
